//: Example MCP server registration. The exact file/format is harness-specific (Claude Code uses .mcp.json / settings). This shows the SHAPE: each server gets a launch command, optional env/auth, and becomes a namespace of tools the agent can call as mcp__<server>__<tool>.

yourcli.//: The highest-value MCP: a thin wrapper around your OWN DevOps CLI. Same commands your engineers run, now callable by the agent with the same safety rails. Mark destructive/long-running tools so the agent warns before firing.

teamcity.//: Your CI. Read build logs, trigger deploys, query 'latest version of X'. A remote server reached over HTTP rather than launched locally.

aws-docs.//: Read-only cloud documentation. Used to VERIFY identifiers, API shapes, region codes before stating them — the antidote to confidently-wrong AWS facts.

atlassian.//: Your issue tracker / wiki. Read a ticket, comment, transition status, search Confluence. Lets the agent work from the same tickets your team does.

notes: Read-only tools (get/list/describe/search) are safe to call freely.
Mutating tools (deploy/create/delete/teardown) must be confirmed with the user first — encode this in the SOUL, not just here.
A tool that isn't connected this session is a capability the agent does NOT have — it should say so and point at how to enable it, not pretend.
Tool schemas can load lazily: hundreds of registered tools cost little until one is actually called.
